title: Two parts of the same thing gone — post-Alexandria one-liner
html:
TL;DR

  • Sept 9, 2019 single-line entry: "Two parts of the same thing gone. It happened and it was like that. ...." — the day before the airplane-flight goodbye-letter to Alexandria in 2019 09 10 low key writing this to end.
  • Written the day Yahir is leaving Phoenix (where he met Alexandria) back toward Oregon for senior year.
  • "Two parts of the same thing" reads as a post breakup style framing without naming the relationship — the elliptical-ellipsis ("....") is the only emotional content.
  • One of the earliest aphorism as feeling container entries — the form here is doing emotional throat-clearing, not list-making.
  • Compare with the explicit goodbye-letter the next day — Sept 9 is the ineffable version, Sept 10 is the structured-pep-talk version.

Key quote

"Two parts of the same thing gone. It happened and it was like that. ...."

Why it matters in the graph

The Sept 9 → Sept 10 pair is the cleanest example in the 2019 corpus of Yahir using the as a two-stage emotional processor: first the ellipsis-laden ineffable hit (Sept 9), then the structured second-person pep-talk (Sept 10). Both are part of the Alexandria processing sequence.

"Two parts of the same thing" is metaphysically vague enough to also read as lineage-coded — the lineage as fate / cyclical time frame later articulated through García Márquez was already pre-loaded as a way of describing endings. Yahir is at the airport between two cities, two states, two relationships — the two parts line lands across all of them at once.

Wikilinks

related.label: Letter to self on a Phoenix→Portland flight, eve of school year
